column4: CARLA (CAR Learning to Act) is one of the most popular autonomous driving simulators globally. Founded in 2016 at the CVC with Intel Labs support; first presented at CoRL 2017. Originally built on Unreal Engine 4; CARLA 0.10.0 (December 2024) migrated to Unreal Engine 5.5. Has an annual CARLA CVPR Autonomous Driving Grand Challenge. Python client API enables scripted scenarios. ScenarioRunner and CARLA-ROS-Bridge are major ecosystem tools.

column4: Installation using prebuilt binaries is simpler than building from source. Requires a discrete GPU (NVIDIA recommended). The Python client installs easily via pip. Building from source requires Unreal Engine 4/5, which is a significant time investment. Headless server mode requires additional Vulkan configuration on Linux.

column4: Server-client architecture requires both server and client to be running; connection failure if server not started first produces clear error messages. Once running, simulation was stable.

column4: Python API is well-designed for scripting scenarios. Supports ROS/ROS 2 integration via CARLA-ROS-Bridge. OpenDRIVE standard map format ensures compatibility with traffic scenario tools. Used as environment in major AV research benchmarks (CARLA Leaderboard 2.0).
